# HVT Documentation

Official documentation for HVT, the auth and access platform behind hvts.app.

Primary docs site: https://docs.hvts.app/
Primary app: https://hvts.app/
Direct API base: https://api.hvts.app/
OpenAPI schema: https://docs.hvts.app/schema.yaml
Sitemap: https://docs.hvts.app/sitemap.xml

## Start here
- https://docs.hvts.app/introduction
- https://docs.hvts.app/quickstart
- https://docs.hvts.app/concepts

## Guides
- https://docs.hvts.app/guides/projects
- https://docs.hvts.app/guides/auth
- https://docs.hvts.app/guides/runtime-roles
- https://docs.hvts.app/guides/sdk
- https://docs.hvts.app/guides/webhooks
- https://docs.hvts.app/guides/audit-logs

## Reference
- https://docs.hvts.app/api
- https://docs.hvts.app/sdk/hvtclient
- https://docs.hvts.app/sdk/auth
- https://docs.hvts.app/sdk/errors

## Notes
- HVT separates platform administration, control-plane organization roles, and project-scoped app roles.
- Runtime access should be enforced by permission slug plus record scope, not by role name alone.
- Runtime sign-up uses /api/v1/auth/runtime/register/ and control-plane sign-up uses /api/v1/auth/register/.
- Managed-service API calls should target https://api.hvts.app, not https://hvts.app.
- Browser runtime auth policy: test keys allow localhost for local development; live keys require the request origin to match the project's runtime frontend URL or configured allowed origins.
